Solution: Biodiversity is the term that is used to define the varieties present in the living organisms on the Earth. This includes plants, animals, microbes, etc. The total number of species that are known and described so far is around 1.7-1.8 million.

Question: The venue and year of the Earth Summit on Conservation of Biodiversity was
Solution: Earth Summit was conducted in 1992 at Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.  It was organized to help take appropriate measures for the conservation of biodiversity and sustainable utilization of its benefits.

Tags.Subject: Biology
Tags.Topic: National and International Measures to Protect Biodiversity

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Topic: National and International Measures to Protect Biodiversity

Question: Which is referred to as ‘Lungs of the Planet Earth’?
Solution: The Amazon rainforest is called ‘Lungs of the planet Earth’ because it covers 2.1 million square miles area with trees that produce 20% of the oxygen of our planet.

Question: The last twenty years alone have witnessed the disappearance of
Solution: Biodiversity is the term used to describe the heterogeneity of all the levels of biological organizations. IUCN keeps the record of species that are facing the threat of extinction. In the past 20 years, 27 species have disappeared from the Earth.

Question: Exotic plant species introduced in India are
Solution: The exotic plant species introduced in India are Lantana camara, water hyacinth, and carrot grass. The introduction of exotic species in the area of endemic species causes invasion in that area. This could be an intentional act by humans or can be accidental. They become the reason for the extinction of endemic species in the introduced area due to competition for food and water and invasive species have no natural predators.

Question: Which one is a hot spot of biodiversity?
Solution: A biodiversity hotspot is the region identified for maximum protection to conserve its very high levels of species richness and high degree of endemism. There are 36 biodiversity hotspots in the world. Three of these hotspots – Western Ghats and Sri Lanka, Indo-Burma and Himalaya–cover our country’s exceptionally high biodiversity regions.

Question: Biodiversity of a geographical region represents
Options: Endangered species found in the region
The diversity of the organisms living in the region
Genetic diversity present in the dominant species of the region
Solution: The biodiversity of a geographical region represents the diversity of organisms present in the region. A particular group of species may have genetic diversity which makes them different from each other. Biodiversity is basically the biological organization of organisms from the unicellular level to the most complex multicellular levels coordinating with each other.

Question:
What is so special about tropics that might account for their greater biological diversity?
i.Unlike temperate regions subjected to frequent glaciations in the past, tropical latitudes have remained relatively undisturbed for millions of years and thus, had a long evolutionary time for species diversification.
ii.Temperate environments, unlike tropical ones, are less seasonal, relatively more constant and predictable. Such constant environments promote niche specialization and lead to a greater species diversity.
iii.There is more solar energy available in the tropics, which contributes to higher productivity; this in turn might contribute indirectly to greater diversity.
Solution: Tropics hold the greatest biodiversity because of constant rainfall and a favorable temperature. Unlike temperate regions subjected to periodic glaciations in the past, tropical latitudes have remained relatively uninterrupted for millions of years and thus, had a long evolutionary time for species diversification. There is more solar energy available in the tropics, which contributes to more productivity; this in turn might contribute indirectly to significant diversity.

Question:
According to IUCN, currently 15,500 species worldwide are facing the threat of extinction. In this regard, match the columns I and II, and choose the correct combination from the options given.
Column I
(Taxonomic group)		Column II
(Threat of extinction)
a.Birds1.12 per cent
b.Amphibians2.23 per cent
c.Mammals3.31 per cent
d.Gymnosperms4.32 per cent
Solution: IUCN keeps the record of species that are facing the threat of extinction. Presently, more than 15,500 species worldwide are facing the threat of extinction. This includes 12% of all bird species, 23% of all mammal species, 32% of all amphibian species, and 31% of all gymnosperm species. The extinction of species is due to habitat loss and climatic changes. Over-exploitation of animals for skin, meat and fur decreases their number and ultimately threatens them to extinction.

Question: The term biodiversity was popularised by
Solution: The term biodiversity was popularised by Edward Wilson. He used this term to describe the immense diversity or heterogeneity which exists at all levels of the biological organization such as genetic diversity, species diversity and ecological diversity.

Question: Which of the following is referred to as “Evil Quartet” with reference to loss of biodiversity?
Options: Habitat loss and fragmentation, over-exploitation, alien species invasion, co-extinction
Habitat destruction, co-extinction, deforestation, species richness
Solution: Biodiversity is the term used to describe the heterogeneity at all the levels of biological organizations. The “Evil Quartet” refers to the four major causes that have led to the destruction of species and loss of biodiversity. These are loss of habitat and fragmentation, over-exploitation, invasion by alien species and co-extinction.

Question: What parameters are used for tiger census in our country’s national parks and sanctuaries?
Solution: In our country, parameters for tiger census in national parks and sanctuaries include both pug marks and fecal pellets. They can be used to determine the number as well as distribution of the tiger population. Newer techniques of excreta DNA analysis and camera trappings are considered more accurate for tiger census.

Question: Who has studied species-area relationship?
Solution: The species-area relationship was studied by Alexander Humboldt. According to his study, the increase in explored area results in an increase in species richness, but only up to a certain limit. The species-area relationship is represented as S = CAz, where S = species richness, A = area, and C and Z are the constants.

Question: Which scientist found that plots with more species showed less year-to-year variation in total biomass?
Solution: David Tilman found that plots with more species showed less year-to-year variation than the plots which had fewer species on the total biomass. The productivity was also high in those plots which had more species of plants.

Question: Lichens are the associations of
Solution: Lichens are associations (mutually beneficial) between fungus and alga. The fungal partner is mycobiont and the algal partner is phycobiont. The mycobiont is usually an ascomycete and the phycobiont is usually green algae. In many ecosystems, they are the pioneer species. The role of the mycobiont is providing body structure and anchorage and absorption of minerals and water. The role of phycobiont is mainly in providing food through photosynthesis.

Question: Gas vacuoles are found in
Solution: Vacuoles are structures which are used to store food materials in the cells. Gas vacuoles, which are found in blue-green, purple and green photosynthetic bacteria, are hollow cylindrical structures and are made up of gas vesicles. These vacuoles inflate and deflate to provide buoyancy and help in floating.

Question: The heterotrophic, eukaryotic, multicellular organism without cell wall; is included in the kingdom
Solution: Animalia is the only kingdom that includes organisms that lack cell walls and are heterotrophic, multicellular eukaryotes. This kingdom includes all the animals such as human beings, cats, dogs, etc.

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Tags.Subject: Biology
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Question: In animals, the stored food reserve is in the form of
Solution: Glycogen is a polysaccharide of glucose. It is the main storage of carbohydrates in animals and acts as a source of energy reserve for the animals.

Tags.Subject: Biology
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Question:
Decomposers like fungi and bacteria are:
i. autotrophs
ii. heterotrophs
iii. saprotrophs
iv. Chemo-autotrophs
Choose the correct answer. (Exemplar)
Solution: Decomposers like bacteria and fungi are heterotrophs because they are dependent on others for their food as they cannot make their own food. They are also saprotrophs because they feed on dead and decaying organic matter.

Question: Fungi Albugo is a member of
Solution: Phycomycetes are defined as the lower fungi which form a non-septate mycelium. These form an obsolete, polyphyletic taxon. Another term for Phycomycetes is zygomycetes. Albugo is a genus that belongs to Phycomycetes.

Question: Euglenoids have their flexible body due to
Solution: In the case of euglenoids that come under the kingdom Protista, there is no cell wall. Instead of a cell wall, there is a protein-rich layer known as pellicle which makes the body of the euglenoids flexible.

Question: Which are smaller than viruses?
Solution: Viroids are smaller than viruses. They are subviral particles having naked RNA without any capsid. Viroids were discovered by T.O. Diener. Bacteria, cyanobacteria, and fungi are much larger in size as compared to viruses.

Question: Who prepares food in lichens?
Solution: Lichen is a composite structure made by a close association of algae (phycobiont) and fungi (mycobiont). Phycobiont synthesize food as it has chlorophyll in them. On the other hand, Mycobiont of fungal filaments provides shelter to the algae and helps absorb nutrients and water with the help of its fine hyphae.

Question: Viruses can multiply in
Solution:
Viruses are acellular particles and do not have their own machinery to carry out metabolic activities. So, they are obligate parasites and need a living host cell to perform functions like multiplication and protein synthesis. Outside the host cell, they are inert particles and do not show any characteristics of living organisms.
-Detritus is the dead matter or waste and is not living.
-Culture media will provide a substrate and nutrients but do not contain any cells in them, hence viruses cannot multiply in it.

Question: Cyanobacteria are
Solution: Cyanobacteria are photosynthetic so they are autotrophs. Cyanobacteria or blue-green algae are free-living and have the ability of nitrogen fixation. E.g., Anabaena, Nostoc, Aulosira, etc. They add organic matter as well as nitrogen to the soil for better yield.

Question: Viroids have
Solution: Viroids have ssRNA without capsids. These are smaller than viruses and have naked RNA. These particles were discovered by T. O. Diener in 1971. He observed a new infectious agent with low molecular weight RNA and named it ‘viroid’.

Question: Who is known as Darwin of the 20th Century?
Solution: Dr. Ernst Mayr is known as the Darwin of the 20th Century. He is so called as he contributed a lot to science. Mayr was one of the leading evolutionary biologist of the era. He was the one who shaped the intellectual watershed known as the evolutionary synthesis, when modern evolutionary biology was formed.

Question: The subunit of the protein coat of viruses is called
Solution: The protein coat of the virus also called capsid consists of numerous small subunits called capsomeres. These capsomeres are arranged in a polyhedral or helical fashion around the nucleic acid and protect them. Its structure and number vary from virus to virus.

Question: Vegetative reproduction in fungi takes place by
Solution: In fungi, vegetative reproduction occurs by fragmentation, budding, and fission. In fragmentation, the mycelium splits into fragments and forms new fungi. Budding is another method of asexual reproduction in which a bud emerges from the parent body and when separated, forms new fungi. Fission is the asexual reproduction in which a cell splits and forms two or more small cells, each of which develops into a separate organism.

Question: Fungi resemble plants in
Options: Lack of chloroplast and heterotrophic nutrition
Having a cell wall and vacuoles
Solution: Like plant cells, fungal cells also possess cell walls and vacuoles. Although the chemical composition is different in plant cell walls (cellulose) and fungal cell walls (chitin).

Question: ________ is the most common method of reproduction in bacteria.
Solution: Bacteria come under the kingdom Monera as they are unicellular and prokaryotic in nature. These organisms mainly divide or reproduce through binary fission, a process of asexual reproduction in which a single parent produces two daughter cells.

Question: Cyanobacteria are used in agricultural fields for crop improvement because they cause
Solution: Cyanobacteria or blue-green algae carry out nitrogen fixation because of the presence of specialized cells known as heterocysts. When introduced in agricultural fields, they fix atmospheric nitrogen. Plants can utilize this fixed nitrogen in the form of nitrates and nitrites. Thus, cyanobacteria are important for crop improvement.

Question: Who made the earliest attempt to a more scientific basis for classification?
Solution: Aristotle was the earliest person to attempt a more scientific basis for the classification of organisms. To classify plants into trees, shrubs, and herbs, he used simple morphological characters. He also divided animals into two groups, those with red blood and those without red blood.

Question: Mycorrhiza represents
Solution: Mycorrhiza is a symbiotic association between fungi and the roots of higher plants. In this association, the fungal partner is benefited as it receives food and shelter. The plant is benefited as it obtains fixed nitrogen and more quantities of minerals and water.

Question:
How many of the following statements regarding fungi are true?
I. Asexual reproduction is common by the formation of spores.
II. Their bodies consist of hyphae that may be interconnected to form mycelium.
III. They secrete digestive enzymes onto organic matter and then absorb the products of the digestion.
IV. Fungi can break down almost any carbon-containing product.
V. Fungi do not enter into symbiotic relationships.
Solution: Except for statement V, all other statements regarding fungi are true. Fungi can form symbiotic associations. Lichen is a symbiotic association of fungi and algae, mycorrhiza is a symbiotic association of fungi with plant roots.

Question: Which is the basis of the division of kingdom fungi?
Solution: Morphology of mycelium, mode of spore formation, and fruiting bodies form the basis of the division of kingdom fungi. On these criteria, fungi are divided into four groups namely, Phycomycetes, Ascomycetes, Basidiomycetes, and Deuteromycetes.

Question: Deuteromycetes members are often moved to ascomycetes or basidiomycetes after the identification of
Solution: The fungi in which only vegetative mode of reproduction is known to occur are known as Deuteromycetes or ‘fungi imperfecti’. The deuteromycetes are moved to ascomycetes or basidiomycetes after the identification of the sexual phase.

Question:
Which of the following microbes can be grown on nutritive media and form colonies?
a. Bacteria
b. Fungi
c. Viruses
d. Viroids
Solution: A media which consists of carbohydrates, nitrogen and other essential requirements for the growth of microorganisms is known as nutrient media. Both bacteria and fungi can be grown on nutrient media by maintaining the correct temperature, pH level, and proper nutrients. Viruses cannot be grown on nutrient media; it requires a living host cell for its replication.

Question: The classification system proposed by Linnaeus was ___________ kingdom system of classification.
Solution: The classification system proposed by Linnaeus was a two-kingdom system of classification. He divided organisms into two kingdoms- one was the Plantae (included all plants), and the other was the Animalia (included all animals).

Question: Recognise the figure and find out the correct matching.
Solution: Both Volvox and Chlamydomonas are green algae that are found in freshwater habitats. Volvox morphologically, structurally, and biologically has been evolved over and over again from Chlamydomonas through convergent evolution. Volvox forms spherical colonies consisting of up to 50000 cells. Chlamydomonas are unicellular, biflagellated green algae.

Question: Viruses cause several diseases in plants and animals. In plants, the symptoms can be
Options: Mosaic formation, dwarfing, and stunted growth
Solution: Viruses lack metabolic machinery to acquire nutrients to generate energy or synthesize proteins. They need host machinery to perform these activities and therefore, infect other organisms. A virus-infected plant can show various symptoms like mosaic formation (discolouration and patching), dwarf and stunted growth, leaf rolling and curling, yellowing and vein clearing, chlorosis, etc.

Question: Which of these is a defining characteristic of plants?
Solution: The defining character of plants is the cellulosic cell wall. Cell wall forms the outermost layer of the plant cell. It is made up of cellulose in the case of plants. The cellulosic cell wall is a feature found only in plant cells. Hence, it is called the defining characteristic of plants.

Tags.Subject: Biology
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Question: Boundaries of which kingdom are not well defined
Solution: The boundaries of the kingdom Protista are not well defined. It includes diatoms, dinoflagellates, euglenoids, slime moulds, and protozoans. What may be a photosynthetic bacteria to one biologist may be a plant to another. This kingdom forms a link with the others dealing with plants, animals, and fungi.

Tags.Subject: Biology
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Question: Deuteromycetes are called ‘fungi imperfecti’ because they have
Solution: The deuteromycetes reproduce by asexual spores named as conidia. Deuteromycetes are called ‘fungi imperfecti’ because the members of this group lack sexual stages. e.g. Alternaria, Collectotrichum, Trichoderma, etc.

Question: With respect to the fungal sexual cycle, choose the correct sequence of events.
Solution: In fungi, sexual reproduction involves the formation and union of two gametes or their nuclei. In fungi, fusion involves the union of cytoplasm as well as nuclei. The former is called plasmogamy while the latter is called karyogamy. In higher fungi, karyogamy is delayed and occurs just before meiosis.

Question: Identify the characteristic that is unique to fungi:
Options: Cell wall of glucans and chitin
Lack of chloroplasts and heterotrophic nutrition
Mannitol, trehalose, and glycogen as storage products
Having haploid nuclei and production of spores
Solution: Cell wall made up of glucans and chitin is a unique    character found only in fungi. Glucan is a polysaccharide derived from D-glucose. Chitin is a long-chain polymer of N-acetylglucosamine, an amide derivative of glucose.

Question:
Match the columns I and II, and choose the correct combination from the options given
Column IColumn II
(Group Protista)(Example)
a.Chrysophytesi.Paramoecium
b.Dinoflagellatesii.Euglena
c.Euglenoidsiii.Gonyaulax
d.Protozoansiv.Diatoms
Solution: Protists are eukaryotic, unicellular organisms. The protists are divided into different groups. Diatoms are members of the chrysophytes group of Protista. The Dinoflagellates group consists of Gonyaulax, Euglenoids group includes organisms like Euglena. Paramecium belongs to the group protozoans of the Protista kingdom.

Question: During unfavourable conditions, slime moulds
Options: form fruiting bodies bearing spores at their tips
Solution: Slime molds are saprophytic protists. In suitable conditions, they form aggregation which is known as plasmodium. This plasmodium in unfavourable condition differentiates and forms fruiting bodies bearing spores at their tips.

Question: Archaebacteria are special since they live in some of the harshest habitats such as
Solution: Archaebacteria are those groups of bacteria that live under harsh climatic conditions. Archaebacteria include thermoacidophiles, halophiles, methanogens, etc. Thermoacidophiles live in hot springs, halophiles in extremely salty areas, and methanogens in marshy areas.

Question: Among the five-kingdom classification, eukaryotes are placed in how many kingdoms?
Solution: Among the five-kingdom classification, the eukaryotes are placed in four out of five kingdoms. These are Protista, Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia hereas Monera is the only kingdom that includes all prokaryotes.

Question: Molecules having charged groups of opposite polarity are
Solution: A molecule containing an equal number of positively and negatively charged functional groups is called a zwitterion. Hence, the net charge is equal to zero. Amino acids are common examples of zwitterions.

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Question: Which of the following statement regarding euglenoids is not true?
Options: Instead of a cell wall, they have a lipid-rich pellicle.
They have two flagella, a short and a long one.
They are photosynthetic in the presence of sunlight but behave like a heterotroph when deprived of sunlight.
Pigments of euglenoids are identical to those present in higher plants.
Solution: The euglenoids lack a cell wall. Instead of a cell wall, they have a flexible layer of protein. It is called the pellicle.

Question: The microscopic organisms that float passively in water currents are termed as
Solution: Planktons are microscopic organisms that flow passively in water currents. These come under chrysophytes under the kingdom Protista.

Question: Contagium vivum fluidum was proposed by
Solution: Martinus William Beijernick (a Dutch microbiologist and botanist) gave a phrase, “Contagium vivum fluidum” (Latin: "contagious living fluid"), to describe a virus. He underlined its (virus's) ability to slip through the finest mesh filters then available, giving it almost liquid properties.

Question: The virus isolated first was
Solution: The virus which was isolated first was the tobacco mosaic virus. It is an ssRNA virus. It was isolated by D. J. Ivanowsky from an infected plant of tobacco with mosaic disease. He found that these particles were smaller than bacteria as they passed through bacteria-proof filters.

Question: Viruses are non-cellular organisms but replicate themselves once they infect the host cell. To which of the following kingdoms do viruses belong?
Solution: A virus is a group of ultramicroscopic, non-cellular, highly infectious agents that multiply only intracellularly inside living host cells without involving growth and division. Viruses are regarded as intermediate between non-living entities and living organisms. It is very difficult to ascertain whether they are living or nonliving. They do not belong to any kingdom.

Question:
Assertion: Sporozoans may have silica shells on their surface.
Reason: Shells of sporozoans help in protection from the acidic environment of the host.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Sporozoans are endoparasites. The body is covered with an elastic pellicle or cuticle. It does not provide any protection.

Question: How many criteria are used in the five-kingdom classification?
Solution: There were five main criteria used by Whittaker to classify organisms in five kingdom classification. These were cell structure, thallus organization, mode of nutrition, reproduction, and phylogenetic relationships.

Question: Members of kingdom Protista are
Solution:
-Protista members are primarily aquatic.
-They have membrane-bound organelles.
-Some of them are ciliated or flagellated.
-They can reproduce by sexual or asexual means.

Question: Which of the following consists of coprophilous members?
Solution: Coprophilous members are mostly ascomycetes. Dung-loving or coprophilous fungi are saprophytic fungi that grow on animal dung. Pilobolus species feed saprophytically on the feces of grazing animals. In fungi, the spores are consumed or taken by herbivores which get excreted along with feces. Fungi germinate and flourish in the feces before releasing their spores to the surrounding area.

Question: Naked cytoplasm, multinucleated, and saprophytic are the characteristics of
Solution: Slime moulds belong to the kingdom Protista. They are multinucleated and have naked cytoplasm. Slime moulds are saprophytic protists. The body moves along decaying twigs and leaves engulfing organic material.

Question:
Assertion: Two kingdom classification was insufficient.
Reason: The majority of organisms did not fall into either of the categories in the two kingdom classification.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Two kingdom classification (founded by Linnaeus) worked well for a long time. But this system did not distinguish between the eukaryotes and prokaryotes, unicellular and multicellular organisms, photosynthetic and non-photosynthetic organisms. Although the classification of organisms (into plants and animals) was easily done and was easy to understand, a large number of organisms did not fall into either category. Hence, the two kingdom classification used for a long time was found inadequate.

Question: Which of the following is NOT a member of Kingdom Protista?
Solution: Chrysophytes include diatoms or golden algae. Dinoflagellates are the protists with two flagella such as Gonyaulax, and slime moulds are saprophytic in nature such as Physarum. All these organisms are the members of kingdom Protista, whereas Deuteromycetes are the members of the Kingdom Fungi.

Question: Cyanobacteria are classified under
Solution: Cyanobacteria are classified under Kingdom Monera as they are prokaryotes. They are generally photosynthetic in nature and contain pigments, chlorophyll a, carotenoids, etc. Nostoc and Oscillatoria are examples of this category.

Question: An important criterion for modern-day classification is
Solution: Modern-day classification is new systematics or biosystematics. It includes a number of physiological and anatomical traits of organisms gathered from the study of different sections like physiology, ecology anatomy, biochemistry, cytology.

Question:
Match the columns I and II, and choose the correct combination from the options given.
Column IColumn II
a.Phycomycetesi.Sac-Fungi
b.Ascomycetesii.Imperfect fungi
c.Basidiomycetesiii.Algal fungi
d.Deuteromycetesiv.Club fungi/ bracket fungi
Solution:
-Phycomycetes are considered to be fungi and because of their morphological similarity with algae, they were for a long time known as ‘algal-fungi’.
-Ascomycetes that belong to the kingdom fungi have sac-like structures which are known as asci which contain spores.
-Fungi having septate hyphae and spores borne on basidium are called basidiomycetes. They include mushrooms, rusts, smuts, and club fungi/ bracket fungi.
-Deuteromycetes are called fungi imperfecti because only the asexual or vegetative phases of these fungi are known.

Question: The two kingdoms that are constant in all types of classifications from Linnaeus to Whittaker are
Solution: Plantae and Animalia are the two kingdoms that were classified by Linnaeus. Since then, although new kingdoms were created from these two kingdoms, these two kingdoms remained constant from the two kingdom systems by Linneaus to the five-kingdom system by R.H. Whittaker.

Question: Some hyphae are continuous tubes filled with multinucleated cytoplasm. These are called
Solution: The long slender tubes which are developed from germinated spores of fungi are called hyphae. Hyphae form an expanding network of mycelium. In some hyphae, continuous aseptate tubes with multinucleated cytoplasm are seen, such hyphae are called coenocytic hyphae.

Question:
Assertion: Virus is an obligate parasite.
Reason: Virus is host-specific.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: The virus is an obligate parasite. It is inert outside the host cell. A virus does not grow, divide, or reproduce like typical organisms. Instead, it multiplies by the independent formation of its parts using host machinery and then assembly of parts to produce virus particles. Viruses are host-specific.

Question: An association between roots of higher plants and fungi is called
Solution: An association between roots of higher plants and fungus is called mycorrhiza. The association is mutually beneficial for both of them. The fungus gets shelter and food from plants. It obtains its nourishment from cortical cells of roots. Fungal hyphae are spread in the soil over a large area. They take part in the absorption of water, dissolving essential minerals in organic debris and providing it to the plant, absorption of inorganic salts from the soil, and in some cases, secreting growth-promoting substances. An example is the roots of Pinus.

Question: A peculiar odor that occurs in marshy areas and cowsheds is due to gas produced by
Solution: A peculiar odor that occurs in marshy areas and cow sheds is due to gas produced by a particular bacteria known as archaebacteria. Archaebacteria are divided into 3 groups- methanogens, halophiles, and thermophiles. Methanogens are basically found in the marshy areas. These methanogens produce biogas which contains 70% methane.

Question: T. O. Diener discovered a/an
Solution: T. O. Diener discovered a sub-viral agent called viroid, having only naked RNA i.e. capsid was absent in them. He found a circular ssRNA that showed intra-strand base pairing. Viroids cause plant diseases. A few examples of viroid diseases include Chrysanthemum stunt, Potato spindle tuber disease, etc.

Question: All eukaryotic unicellular organisms belong to
Solution: The kingdom Protista (Gk. Protistos = first of all) was proposed by Ernst Haeckel (1866). All single-celled eukaryotes are placed in the kingdom Protista. Phylogenetically, the kingdom Protista acts as a connecting link between the prokaryotic kingdom- Monera on one hand and the complex multicellular kingdoms– Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia on the other hand. Protista is regarded as the ancestors of all multicellular eukaryotic organisms.

Question: Mycorrhiza exhibits the phenomenon of
Solution: Mycorrhiza is defined as the association between the roots of higher plants and fungus. Fungi protect the plant from pathogens and also increase the surface area through which plants can absorb soil nutrients and water. Plants provide food to the fungi. In this way, both plants and fungi are benefited from this association. Hence, it is called symbiosis.

Question: Archaebacteria differ from eubacteria in
Solution: Archaebacteria differ from eubacteria due to the presence of certain special compounds in their cell wall which helps them to survive in extreme climatic conditions. The cell wall of archaebacteria is made up of polysaccharides and proteins but lacks peptidoglycan whereas the eubacterial cell wall contains peptidoglycan.

Question: Fungi grow in
Solution: Fungi are unicellular or multicellular, eukaryotic spore-forming organisms. e.g. yeast, molds, mushrooms, etc. Fungi grow in warm and humid places.

Question: A dikaryon is formed when
Options: the two haploid cells do not fuse immediately
Solution: Dikaryon is a stage/phase of a cell, in which there are two nuclei. This condition arises if the fusion of cytoplasm (plasmogamy) is not immediately followed by fusion of nuclei (karyogamy), i.e., there is a delay between them. Such cells are called dikaryotic cells. This condition is found in higher fungi where karyogamy is delayed (with respect to plasmogamy) and is called dikaryophase.

Question: Which types of bacteria are responsible for the production of biogas?
Solution: Methanogens are the type of archaebacteria that are ultimately responsible for the production of biogas from the dung of animals such as cows, buffaloes, etc. These organisms are present in the gut of ruminant organisms such as cows, buffalo, etc.

Question: Which of the following classes of fungi are characterized by the presence of coenocytic, multinucleate, and branched mycelium?
Solution: The long slender tubes which develop from germinated spores of fungi are called hyphae. Hyphae form a radially expanding network known as mycelium. In some hyphae, continuous tubes with multinucleated cytoplasm are seen which is known as coenocytic mycelium. The presence of coenocytic multinucleate, aseptate, and branched mycelium is a characteristic feature of Phycomycetes.

Question: Proteinaceous infectious agents are called
Solution: Proteinaceous infectious agents that cause disease are called prions. It causes infection both in humans and animals. They are like viruses but they lack nucleic acid. Prions trigger the proteins to fold in an abnormal way.

Question:
Assertion: Brown algae vary from olive green to brown in color.
Reason: Fucoxanthin is responsible for color variation in brown algae.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Brown algae vary in color from olive green to various shades of brown depending upon the amount of the xanthophyll pigment, fucoxanthin present in them.

Question: Given figure is of filamentous blue-green alga Nostoc. Identify the parts marked as A and B and select the correct option
Solution: In the given figure of Nostoc, the label ‘A’ is heterocyst. It is a specialized cell that can fix atmospheric nitrogen. The label ‘B’ is the mucilaginous sheath that is present on the surface of the organism.

Question: Which of the following combinations of characters is true for slime moulds?
Options: Parasitic, plasmodium with true walls, spores dispersed by air currents
Saprophytic, plasmodium without walls, spores dispersed by water
Parasitic, plasmodium	without walls, spores dispersed by water
Saprophytic, plasmodium without walls, spores dispersed by air currents
Solution: Slime molds are saprophytic protists. In suitable conditions, they form aggregation which is known as plasmodium. Their vegetative stage is formed by a mass of cellular material without cell dividers hence does not contain a cell wall. In unfavourable conditions, they form haploid spores that get scattered through air currents.

Question:
Consider the following characters
I. Heterotrophic organisms
II. A dikaryon stage
III. Cell wall made of chitin
IV. Undergo nuclear mitosis
Which of the above relate to fungi?
Solution:
All these characters mentioned are related to fungi.
-They are heterotrophic organisms, i.e., they depend on other organisms or dead organic matter for nutrition.
-They can exhibit a dikaryon stage, where each cell has two nuclei (n + n).
-Their cell wall is made of chitin.
-They can undergo nuclear mitosis.

Question:
Match different organisms in Column-I with their uses in Column-II and select the correct answer from the codes given below.
Column-IColumn-II
A. Lactobacillus (i) Formation of
acidophilus dough
B. Saccharomyces (ii) Single cell
cerevisiaeproteins
C. Propionibacterium(iii) Conversion of
shermaniimilk into curd
D. Spirulina(iv) Formation of
Swiss cheese
Solution: Lactobacillus acidophilus is a bacteria that helps in the conversion of milk into curd. Saccharomyces cerevisiae is a type of yeast that helps in the formation of dough. Propionibacterium shermanii is a type of bacteria that helps in the production of Swiss cheese. Spirulina is a type of cyanobacteria and is an excellent source of single-cell protein.

Question: Chloroplast of Chlamydomonas is
Solution: Chlamydomonas is a subtype of green algae which consists of prominent, single, cup-shaped chloroplasts. The chloroplast contains a single pyrenoid for starch formation during photosynthesis.

Question: Fusion of protoplasms between two fungal gametes is called
Solution: The fusion of protoplasms between two fungal gametes is called plasmogamy. In plasmogamy, the cytoplasm of two fungal gametes fuses without fusion of nuclei, which brings two haploid nuclei into a single cell.

Question: Which of the following organisms has been placed under kingdom Protista?
Solution: Chrysophytes such as diatoms and golden algae; dinoflagellates such as Gonyaulax; euglenoids such as Euglena; slime moulds such as Physarum; protozoans such as Entamoeba, Plasmodium, Leishmania, etc., all come under the kingdom Protista. These organisms are unicellular and eukaryotic.

Question: Absorptive heterotrophic nutrition is exhibited by
Solution: Nutrition in which the organisms depend upon another organism for food to survive is known as heterotrophic nutrition. These organisms take all the organic materials which are required to survive them. The eumycetes or true fungi are achlorophyllous. They absorb the food into their hyphae from the surroundings.

Question: Fungi and bacteria, which feed on dead organic matter are called
Solution: Saprophytes are defined as the organisms which get their food from decaying and dead organic matter. Saprophytic fungi and bacteria are very useful for us in our gardens as they help in breaking down the complex organic matters into minerals and nutrients needed for the plants. They also clean the environment in this process.

Question: Association of roots of higher plants and fungi is
Solution: A symbiotic association between roots of higher plants and fungi is called mycorrhizae. The mycorrhiza may form ectomycorrhizal or endomycorrhizal association.

Question:
Assertion: Euglena is called a plant-animal.
Reason: Pellicle of Euglena is made up of cellulose and not protein.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Euglena is studied as a plant as well as an animal. It is called a plant-animal. Plant characteristics of Euglena are the presence of chloroplasts with chlorophyll and holophytic (photosynthetic) nutrition in presence of light. Animal characters of Euglena are the presence of pellicle which is made up of proteins and not of cellulose, presence of stigma and paraflagellar body (photosensitive structures), presence of contractile vacuole (not found in plants) and presence of longitudinal binary fission.

Question: Which bacteria are most abundant in nature?
Solution: The bacteria which are most abundant in nature are heterotrophic bacteria. These bacteria do not synthesize their own food. Hence, they are called heterotrophic bacteria.

Question: Which type of bacteria plays a great role in recycling nutrients?
Solution: Chemosynthetic autotrophic bacteria play a crucial role in the recycling of nutrients and various inorganic substances such as nitrates, nitrites, and ammonia. They help to accelerate the breakdown and recycling of these complex substances.

Question: Sole members of kingdom Monera are
Solution: Bacteria are the sole members of the kingdom Monera. The kingdom monera includes all unicellular prokaryotic organisms.

Question: Cyanobacteria are classified under which of the following kingdoms?
Solution: Cyanobacteria or blue-green algae are classified as eubacteria in the kingdom monera as per the five-kingdom classification system given by Whittaker. These organisms are prokaryotic unicellular organisms that are photosynthetic autotrophs due to the presence of chlorophyll-a.

Question: In the five-kingdom classification, Euglena is placed in
Solution: In the five-kingdom classification as proposed by R. H. Whittaker, Euglena is classified under euglenoids which fall under the kingdom Protista. These are unicellular, eukaryotic organisms.

Question: Fungi producing eight spores in a sac belong to the class
Solution: Ascomycetes have sac-like structures which are known as asci which contain spores. In most ascomycetes, eight spores are formed in each ascus (pl. asci) by sexual reproduction. In fungi, spores are often classified based on the structure in which meiosis and spore formation take place.

Question: Under suitable conditions slime moulds form
Solution: Slime molds are saprophytic protists. Under suitable conditions, they form aggregation which is known as plasmodium. In unfavourable conditions, this plasmodium differentiates and forms fruiting bodies.

Question:
Assertion: Methanogens are present in the gut of several ruminant animals.
Reason: Methanogens help in the production of methane from dung of ruminants.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Methanogens occur in marshy areas where they convert formic acid and carbon dioxide into methane with the help of hydrogen. Some of the methanogen archaebacteria live as symbionts (e.g., Methanobacterium) inside rumen or first chamber in the stomach of herbivorous animals that chew their cud (ruminants, e.g., cow, buffalo) and they help in production of methane from dung of these animals. These archaebacteria are helpful to the ruminants in fermentation of cellulose.

Question: The edible ascomycetes are
Solution: The edible fungi are morels and truffles. Both are the members of ascomycetes or sac fungi. Certain mushrooms or Agaricus are also edible but they belong to class basidiomycetes.

Question: Chlorophyll-a is present in
Solution: Cyanobacteria or blue-green algae are unicellular, prokaryotic, eubacteria belonging to the kingdom monera. These bacteria are photosynthetic and have chlorophyll-a similar to green plants.

Question:
Dinoflagellates,
I. Are mostly marine and photosynthetic
II. Have a cell wall that has stiff cellulose plates on the outer surface.
III. Have two flagella
IV. Have chloroplasts bound by three membranes.
The correct statements are
Solution:
-Dinoflagellates are mostly marine and photosynthetic.
-They have a cell wall that has stiff cellulose plates on the outer surface.
-They have two flagella
-They have chloroplasts bound by three membranes.
All these statements regarding dinoflagellates are correct.

Question:
Assertion: Cell walls of chrysophytes are indestructible.
Reason: Cell walls of chrysophytes have a layer of magnesium pectate embedded in it.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Chrysophytes include diatoms and golden algae (desmids). They are found in freshwater as well as in marine environments. The cell walls are embedded with silica and thus the walls are indestructible.

Question: The deuteromycetes reproduce by asexual spores named as
Solution: The deuteromycetes reproduce by asexual spores named as conidia. Deuteromycetes are called ‘fungi imperfecti’ because the members of this group lack sexual stages, e.g., Alternaria, Colletotrichum, Trichoderma, etc.

Question: Coprophilous fungus grows
Solution: Coprophilous fungi are also known as dung-loving and grow on animal dung. Pilobolus species feed saprophytically on the feces of grazing animals. Spores of the fungi are consumed or taken by herbivores and get excreted along with feces. Fungi germinate and flourish in the feces before releasing their spores to the surrounding area.

Question: In the five-kingdom classification, lichens are placed in which kingdom?
Solution: Lichens are formed by the symbiotic association between algae and fungi. Hence, these organisms cannot be placed in any of the kingdoms in the five-kingdom classification proposed by R.H. Whittaker.

Question: The main purpose of classification of organisms is to
Solution: The main purpose of the classification of organisms is to group them based on their resemblances and differences with other organisms. This enables the identification of organisms easily and helps in the easy study of organisms. Classification establishes a relationship among different organisms based on evolutionary, morphological, anatomical, physiological, biochemical, and many other criteria.

Question: Which has a cavity (gullet) that opens to the outside of the cell surface?
Solution: The single-celled freshwater animal which has a slipper-like shape and is covered with cilia is known as Paramecium. It has a cavity (gullet) that opens to the outside of the cell surface. The food is taken in by Paramecium through the gullet.

Question:
Assertion: Chemosynthetic autotrophic bacteria oxidize various inorganic substances.
Reason: Energy released during oxidation is used in ATP production.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Chemoautotrophic bacteria are able to manufacture their organic food from inorganic raw materials with the help of energy derived from exergonic chemical reactions involving oxidation of an inorganic substance present in the external medium. The chemical energy obtained from oxidation reactions is trapped in ATP molecules.

Question: Which of the following is considered as neither prokaryotes nor eukaryotes?
Solution: Bacteriophages are acellular in nature as they are viruses infecting bacterial cells. They do not have protoplasm or other living structures but have proteins and nucleic acids. They do not contain ribosomes also. Hence, they can neither be considered prokaryotes nor eukaryotes.

Question:
Assertion: Classification is necessary to study all living organisms.
Reason: In Classification, individuals are grouped into categories.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Diversity of living organisms makes it impossible to study all. So, for making study convenient, Classification process is used. In Classification, individuals are grouped into various categories. Classification makes the study of characteristics of living organisms, according to their categories, possible.

Question: What is true about archaebacteria?
Solution: Archaebacteria are those bacteria that are considered the oldest living beings. They come under the kingdom monera. These types of bacteria are found in the harshest climatic conditions such as extremely salted areas, hot springs, etc. These bacteria have been living in such extreme conditions since ancient times. Therefore they are known as the oldest living beings.

Question:
Match Column-I with Column-Il and select the correct answer from the codes given below.
Column-IColumn-II
A.Methanogens(i) BOD
B. Fermenters(ii) Methane rich fuel gas
C. Organic waste in water(iii) Production of methane
D. Biogas (iv) Large vessels forgrowing microbes
Solution: Methanogens help in the production of methane gas. Fermenters are the large vessels for growing microorganisms. Biological oxygen demand (BOD) is the amount of oxygen required for the breakdown of organic waste in the water by biological entities like microorganisms at a particular temperature. Biogas is a methane-rich fuel that is produced by the decomposition of bio-wastes with the help of methanogens in anaerobic condition.

Question:
Assertion: Mycoplasmas are pathogenic in animals and plants.
Reason: Mycoplasmas lack a cell wall and can survive without oxygen.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Mycoplasma or mollicutes are the simplest and the smallest living prokaryotes and can survive without oxygen. They completely lack a cell wall. Many mycoplasma are pathogenic in animals and plants. Majority of them are parasitic due to their inability to synthesise required growth factors.

Question:
Assertion: Archaebacteria are able to survive in harsh habitats.
Reason: Presence of peptidoglycan in the cell wall helps archaebacteria to survive in extreme conditions.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Archaebacteria are special since they live in some of the harshest habitats such as extreme salty areas (halophiles), hot springs (thermoacidophiles) and marshy areas (methanogens). Archaebacteria differ from other bacteria in having a different cell wall structure and this feature is responsible for their survival in extreme conditions. Archaebacteria are characterised by absence of peptidoglycan in their cell walls. Instead, the cell wall contains protein and non-cellulosic polysaccharide.

Question: In fungi, heterotrophic nutrition may be
Solution: Plants and animals have different modes of nutrition. Nutrition in which the organisms depend upon other organisms for food to survive is known as heterotrophic nutrition. Fungi are heterotrophic. Saprophytic fungi feed on dead organic matter. Some fungi may act as parasites and feed on their host. Fungi may form a symbiotic association with an autotrophic organism like plant roots and obtain the food from the plant.

Question: Which of the following is a photosynthetic nitrogen-fixer?
Solution: Nostoc, Anabaena and Oscillatoria – all are nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria. Cyanobacteria are photosynthetic in nature.

Question: Which of the following is a defining character of plants?
Solution:
-Plants are autotrophic in nature, but members of other Kingdoms also show autotrophic nutrition.
-Plants have eukaryotic cell structures, members of Protista, Fungi and Animalia also have eukaryotic structures. Kingdom Monera has members with prokaryotic cell structures.
-Plant cell walls are mainly made of cellulose.
-Aerobic respiration is exhibited by members of other Kingdoms too.

Tags.Subject: Biology
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Question: Select the pair that consists of viral diseases.
Solution: Mumps, smallpox, herpes, and influenza are viral diseases. Paramyxovirus causes mumps. Infection of variola virus results in smallpox. HSV or herpes simplex virus causes herpes disease. Diseases like influenza and the common cold are caused by the influenza virus. Syphilis is a sexually transmitted disease caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum. Pneumonia can occur due to bacterial, fungal, or viral infections but usually, the bacterium Streptococcus pneumoniae is involved in it.

Question: Which disease is caused by viroids?
Solution: Potato spindle tuber disease is caused by viroid which results in malformed tubers and stunted growth of the plant. Leaf curling disease causes curled and discoloured leaves in plants and usually results in infection by the fungus. Dwarfing disease makes the plants short in height like Barley dwarf disease. It is not caused by viroids.

Question: All the following statements regarding basidiomycetes are correct except
Options: Asexual spores and vegetative reproduction are generally not found.
Basidiospores are exogenously produced on the basidium.
Solution: Asexual reproduction is generally not found in basidiomycetes, but vegetative reproduction by fragmentation is common.

Question:
Assertion: Algae show only anisogamous type of reproduction.
Reason: In algae, gametes can never be non-flagellated.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Sexual reproduction in algae takes place through fusion of two gametes. These gametes can be flagellated and similar in size (as in Chlamydomonas) or non-flagellated (non- motile) but similar in size (as in Spirogyra). Such reproduction is called isogamous. Fusion of two male gametes dissimilar in size is termed as anisogamous. Fusion between one large non-motile female gamete and a smaller, motile male gamete is oogamous.

Question: Kingdom Monera comprises
Solution: All the members of kingdom monera are prokaryotic unicellular organisms. Blue-green algae (cyanobacteria) such as Nostoc or Anabaena; archaebacteria such as halophiles, methanogens, thermoacidophiles, and the smallest bacteria mycoplasma, all come under the kingdom Monera.

Question: Microbes which act as biofertilizers are
Options: Mycorrhiza formed by the algae of the genus Glomus
Root nodule bacteria-Azotobacter fix atmospheric nitrogen in paddy fields
Solution: Free-living nitrogen-fixing bacteria live freely in the soil and perform nitrogen fixation, for example, Azospirillum. Some are saprotrophic which live on organic remains, e.g., Azotobacter, Bacillus polymyxa, Clostridium beijerinckia. Azotobacter occurring in the fields of cotton, maize and rice increases yield.

Question: Five kingdom classification was based on
Solution: Cell structure, thallus organization, and mode of nutrition, all form the basic criteria of the five-kingdom classification. All these characteristics vary from kingdom to kingdom such as green plants have cell walls of cellulose, fungi have cell walls of chitin, but animals lack a cell wall. Plants show an autotrophic mode of nutrition and animals are heterotrophic in which most of them are holozoic or parasitic.

Question: The five-kingdom classification was proposed by
Solution: R. H. Whittaker was the person who proposed the five-kingdom classification in 1969. He divided the organisms into five kingdoms- Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia. The main criteria for classification used by him include cell structure, thallus organization, mode of nutrition, reproduction, and phylogenetic relationships.

Question: The protists have
Options: Membrane-bound nucleoproteins lying embedded in the cytoplasm.
Nucleoprotein in direct contact with the rest of the cell substance.
Solution:
-The protists have membrane-bound nucleoproteins lying embedded in the cytoplasm.
-They are eukaryotic organisms, so they don't have free nucleic acid aggregates.
-The nucleoprotein is not in direct contact with the cytoplasm.

Question: Saprophytic protists are
Solution: Slime moulds are a group of protists which thrive on dead and decaying matter. Hence, they are saprophytic.

Question: Identify the incorrect statement:
Options: Virus has a protein coat called capsid made of small subunit called capsomere
The core has the genetic material either RNA or DNA or both
The capsomers are arranged in helical or icosahedral forms
Virus can infect any type of organism
Solution: The central portion of the virus which contains nucleic acid is known as the core.  The virus has genetic material either DNA or RNA but not both.

Question: The non-living characteristic of a virus is
Options: It can multiply only inside the host
It can cause diseases in the host
Solution: The ability to multiply, undergo mutation, and cause disease in the host cell are living characteristics of viruses and they are also shown by other living cells and microorganisms like bacteria and protozoa. The crystallization of viruses is a non-living feature. The crystallization of viruses was first shown by W. M. Stanley. These particles were inert outside the host cell and could be crystallized.

Question: Presence of which is not true for Euglena?
Solution: Euglenoids are eukaryotic microorganisms that are single-celled. They consist of the characteristics of both plants and animals. In the presence of sunlight, Euglena uses chloroplasts for photosynthesis which consists of the pigments chlorophyll a and b. The cell of Euglena is enclosed by a semi-rigid pellicle which is made up of a protein sheath; it lacks the cell wall. Euglena engulfs the food particles by phagocytosis.

Question: Coenocytic mycelium is
Solution: The long slender tubes which develop from germinated spores of fungi are called hyphae. Hyphae form a radially expanding network known as mycelium. In some hyphae, continuous tubes with multinucleated cytoplasm are seen which is known as coenocytic mycelium.

Question: Organisms living in salty areas are called as
Solution: Halophiles are a type of archaebacteria that occur in salt-rich substrata (2.5 - 5.0 M) like salt pans, salt beds, and salt marshes, e.g., Halobacterium, Halococcus. They are aerobic, chemoheterotrophs. Their cell membranes have red carotenoid pigment for protection against harmful solar radiation.

Question: Artificial system of classification was first used by
Solution: Artificial system of classification is one in which only morphological features are taken into account. This was first proposed by Carl Linnaeus.

Question:
Assertion: Deuteromycetes are known as fungi imperfecti.
Reason: In Deuteromycetes, only the asexual phase is known.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Deuteromycetes are commonly known as imperfect fungi because only the asexual or vegetative phases of these fungi are known and the perfect or sexual stage is either absent or not known.

Question: The difference between virus and viroid is
Options: absence of protein coat in viroid but present in virus
presence of low molecular weight RNA in virus but absent in viroid
Solution: Viroids are the smallest known agent of infectious diseases. They lack capsid as well as proteins (that are present in viruses) and consist of only single-stranded RNA. The RNA of the viroid is of low molecular weight.

Question:
Assertion: In lichens, mycobiont and phycobiont are symbiotically associated in which algae is predominant and fungi is a subordinate partner.
Reason: The fungus provides food and alga protects the fungus from unfavourable conditions.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Lichens are symbiotic associations i.e., mutually beneficial associations between fungus and alga. The fungal partner is called mycobiont and the algal partner is called phycobiont, which are heterotrophic and autotrophic, respectively. Algae prepare food for fungi and fungi provide shelter and absorb mineral nutrients and water for their partner. Fungi dominate the relationship.

Question: Diatoms have left behind a large amount of cell wall deposits in their habitat because:
Options: They are most abundant in that habitat.
They secrete a persistent substance from their cell.
Solution: The cell walls of diatoms are made up of silica. It is an indestructible material. Hence, remains are deposited at the seafloor after the death of diatoms.

Question: In viruses, which of the following act as genetic material?
Solution: Viruses have nucleic acids covered with a proteinaceous coat to protect the genetic material. They can either have DNA or RNA as their genetic material. DNA or RNA can be single-stranded (ssRNA, ssDNA) or double-stranded (dsRNA, dsDNA). Examples of ssRNA viruses are tobacco mosaic virus, HIV virus and dsRNA viruses are mycophages, diarrhea virus. Adenovirus is an ssDNA virus and examples of dsDNA viruses are T-phage, herpes virus, etc.

Question: Which of the following groups of organisms are included under chrysophytes?
Solution:
-Diatoms and desmids (golden algae) are included under Chrysophytes which are the members of the kingdom Protista.
-Kingdom Protista is divided into Chrysophytes, Dinoflagellates (e.g., Gonyaulax), euglenoids (e.g., Euglena), slime moulds, and protozoa (e.g., Amoeba).

Question:
Assertion: Phycomycetes are commonly known as sac fungi.
Reason: In Phycomycetes, ascopore (sexual spores) are produced endogenously in sac-like asci
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Ascomycetes are commonly known as sac-fungi. Sexual spores of Ascomycetes are produced endogenously in sac-like asci. Members of Phycomycetes are found in aquatic habitats and on decaying wood in moist and damp places or as obligate parasites on plants.

Question: Fungi without sexual or perfect stages are classified as
Solution: Fungi without sexual or perfect stages are classified as deuteromycetes. These are also known as ‘fungi imperfecti’ because only the asexual or vegetative phases of these fungi are known. When the sexual forms of these fungi were discovered they were moved into classes they rightly belong to.

Question: Which of the following characters serve as the criteria for the five-kingdom system of classification as used by R. H. Whittaker?
Solution: Cell structure and thallus organization are the criteria that vary from kingdom to kingdom. Plants have a cell wall made of cellulose whereas animals lack a cell wall. Similarly, cell structure, thallus organization, mode of nutrition, reproduction, and phylogenetic relationship are the characters that serve as the basic criteria for the classification of organisms.

Question: Lichen is an association between
Solution: Lichens are symbiotic associations i.e. mutually useful associations, between algae and fungi. The algal component is known as phycobiont and the fungal component as mycobiont, which are autotrophic and heterotrophic, respectively. The fungal partner is usually an ascomycete. The algal partner is usually a green alga. Algae prepare food for fungi and fungi provide shelter and absorb mineral nutrients and water for their partner.

Question:
Recognise the figure and find out the correct match.
a.i.Bacilli
b.ii.Cocci
c.iii.Spirilla
d.ivVibrio
Solution: In the above figure, the comma-shaped bacteria are known as Vibrio, the rod-shaped bacteria are known as Bacilli, the spherical bacteria are known as Cocci and the spiral-shaped bacteria are known as Spirilla. All these bacteria are unicellular and prokaryotic and come under the kingdom monera.

Question: Protists are primarily ______ in nature.
Solution: All the organisms that are included in the kingdom Protista are primarily aquatic in nature. Examples- Euglenoids, dinoflagellates, chrysophytes, etc. Protists are unicellular, eukaryotic organisms.

Question: Virus envelope is known as
Solution: The nucleic acids of viruses (DNA or RNA) are surrounded by a proteinaceous coat called the capsid. These capsids have numerous subunits called capsomeres which are arranged in a helical or polyhedral fashion. They perform the function of protection of the genetic material.

Question:
Members of Phycomycetes are found in
(i) aquatic habitats
(ii) on decaying wood
(iii) moist and damp places
(iv) as obligate parasites on plants.
Choose from the following options.
Solution: Phycomycetes are the group of fungi characterised by septate and coenocytic mycelium. They can live in a wide variety of habitats. They can be aquatic, saprotrophic, parasitic or could be living in moist and damp places. Some examples of phycomycetes are Rhizopus (black bread mold), Mucor (dung mold), Albugo (parasitic).

Question:
Assertion: Red color of Rhodophyta is due to the abundant formation of r-phycoerythrin.
Reason: r-Phycoerythrin is able to absorb blue-green wavelengths of light and reflect a red color.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: The red color of red algae (Rhodophyta) is due to the abundant formation of r-phycoerythrin. Phycoerythrin absorbs blue-green wavelengths of light and reflects red light and thus imparts red color to algae.

Question: Nostoc and Anabaena can fix atmospheric nitrogen in specialized cells known as
Solution: Nostoc and Anabaena are cyanobacteria or blue-green algae. They fix the atmospheric nitrogen with the help of special cells present in their body which are known as heterocysts.

Question: Which of the following organisms completely lack a cell wall, they are the smallest living cells known and can survive without oxygen?
Solution: Mycoplasma is the smallest living cell that can survive without oxygen. They are prokaryotic in nature and completely lack cell walls. They come under the kingdom Monera. Euglenoids and slime moulds are eukaryotic protists.

Question: Floridean starch is produced by
Solution: Red algae store their food as floridean starch. It is a type of storage glucan (a polymer of glucose).

Question:
Assertion: Cyanobacteria are photosynthetic autotrophs.
Reason: Cyanobacteria have chlorophyll a and b similar to green plants.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
Solution: Cyanobacteria (also referred to as blue-green algae) are photosynthetic autotrophs. They have only chlorophyll-a similar to green plants.

Question: Which one does not belong to Monera?
Solution: Mycoplasma, eubacteria or true bacteria, and archaebacteria such as halophiles, thermoacidophiles, and methanogens, all are prokaryotic bacteria that come under the kingdom Monera. Whereas, slime moulds are eukaryotic organisms that come under the kingdom Protista.

Question: The cell wall is composed of two thin overlapping shells which fit together like a soap case, in
Solution: Diatoms that come under the group chrysophytes in the kingdom Protista have a cell wall composed of two thin overlapping shells. These shells (frustules) fit together like a soap case. These walls are embedded with silica and hence are indestructible. The accumulation of these walls over billions of years had resulted in the formation of the diatomaceous earth at the bottom of the sea.

Question: Indefinite growth of multicellular body throughout the life is a characteristic feature of the kingdom
Solution: Plants generally have the ability to continue to grow in height throughout their life. In animals, the growth is definite and it stops after a certain age.

Tags.Subject: Biology
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Topic: Introduction to Kingdom Plantae and Animalia

Question: The given fungus belongs to which class
Solution: The diagram shows the fungus of the genus Agaricus. It is commonly known as mushroom. It is a member of basidiomycetes. The fruiting body is related to reproduction and is situated erectly above the soil. It consists of an umbrella-like pileus and a stem-like stipe.

Question: All eukaryotic unicellular organisms belong to
Solution: All the unicellular eukaryotic organisms belong to the kingdom Protista, which includes chrysophytes, dinoflagellates, euglenoids, slime moulds, etc. whereas all the unicellular, prokaryotic organisms belong to the kingdom monera.

Question: Sleeping sickness is caused by
Solution: African trypanosomiasis also known as sleeping sickness is a disease caused by a flagellated protozoan. Trypanosoma gambiense causes sleeping sickness. This parasite is transmitted by a blood-sucking tsetse fly. The common symptoms include fever, headache, extreme fatigue, aching muscles and joints, swollen lymph nodes, and irritability. Few people may also develop skin rashes.

Question: Which of the following bacteria are comma-shaped?
Solution: The comma-shaped bacteria are known as vibrium (pl. vibrio). Vibrio cholerae is the causative agent of the disease cholera.

Question: The main storage carbohydrate of fungi is
Solution: Fungi are much more closely related to animals than plants. Plants store carbohydrates in the form of starch. Glycogen is a polysaccharide of glucose. It is the main storage of carbohydrates and energy reserves in animals. Reserved food material for the fungi is in the form of glycogen and oil.

Question: The practical purpose of classification of living organisms is to
Options: Explain the origin of living organisms
Trace the evolution of living organisms
Solution: Biological classification is the scientific arrangement of organisms in a hierarchical series of groups and subgroups on the basis of similarities and differences in their traits. It helps in building evolutionary pathways and in identifying new organisms.

Question: Mushrooms, bracket fungi, or puffballs are placed in
Solution: Fungi having septate hyphae and spores borne on basidium are called basidiomycetes. Mushrooms, rusts, smuts, and puffballs are examples of basidiomycetes.

Question: Methanogenic bacteria are not found in the
Solution: Methanogens are a type of archaebacteria that are basically found in marshy areas with an anaerobic environment. Methanogens are not present in the activated sludge but present in the gobar gas plant and rumen of the cattle. These methanogens help in the production of biogas and are also found in waterlogged paddy fields. Activated sludge is acted upon by aerobic bacteria.

Question: Archaebacteria are the special type of bacteria since they live in some of the harshest habitats such as hot springs are called
Solution: Thermoacidophiles are the special type of archaebacteria that are able to live in harsh climatic conditions such as hot springs and acidic conditions. Special structural and biochemical adaptations make it possible for these organisms to survive.

Question: Identify the incorrect statement regarding eubacteria as a group
Options: Chemosynthetic autotrophic bacteria play a great role in recycling nutrients like nitrogen, phosphorus, iron, and sulphur.
Heterotrophic bacteria are the most abundant in nature.
Bacteria reproduce mainly by fission but sometimes under favourable conditions, they produce spores.
Solution: Statement in the option (d) is incorrect. Bacteria reproduce mainly by fission during favourable conditions. Under unfavourable conditions, they produce spores.

Question: Association between Mycobiont and Phycobiont is found in
Solution: Algae and fungi show a mutual relationship with each other and form lichens. The fungal component of this structure is called mycobiont and the algal component is known as phycobiont. Mycobiont helps the plant to absorb minerals and provide shelter to the algae whereas phycobiont contains chloroplasts and prepares food for the plant. Lichens can have varied structures like crustose, fruticose, etc.

Question: Identify the incorrectly matched pair
Options: slime moulds- spore dispersal by water
Solution: In slime moulds, under unfavourable conditions, spores are formed. These spores are dispersed by air.

Question: The network of hyphae is known as
Solution: The long slender tubes which are developed from germinated spores are called hyphae. Hyphae are the filaments whose branches form a radially expanding network known as mycelium. This develops the thallus or an undifferentiated body of the typical fungus.

Question: Bacteria show a range in the number of arrangement of flagella. Bacterial flagellum is composed of
Options: Three parts – filament, hook and basement membrane
Three parts – filament, shaft and basal body
Three parts – filament, hook and basal body
Solution: The flagellum is a whip-like structure that helps with bacterial motility. It consists of 3 parts. The filament is the long helical structure made of the protein flagellin. The basal body functions as the motor while the hook connects the filament to the basal body.

Question: The inorganic compounds like sulphate, phosphate, etc., are found in
Solution: When the tissue is dried and fully burnt, all the carbon compounds are oxidized to gaseous forms - carbon dioxide and water vapor which evaporates. What remains is called ash, which contains inorganic elements. These elements form inorganic compounds like sulphate, phosphate, etc. Sulphate and phosphate are part of the chemical composition of the cytoplasm and are included in the acid-soluble pool.

Question: Which of the following groups contains all polysaccharides?
Solution:
In option (a), only glycogen is a polysaccharide. In options (b) and (c), none of the given sugars are polysaccharides.
In option (d), glycogen, cellulose, and starch are all polysaccharides.

Question: Denaturation of proteins leads to loss of its biological activity by
Options: Loss of both primary and secondary structures
Loss of both secondary and tertiary structures
Solution:
Denaturation of proteins involves the destruction of both the secondary structure of protein and the tertiary structure of protein, but these reactions are not strong enough to break the peptide bonds and the primary structures remains same.
Hence, the correct answer is option (d)

Question: A fatty acid has a carboxyl group attached to an R group. The number of carbon atoms in R group of a fatty acid may be
Solution: A fatty acid has a carboxyl group attached to an R group. The number of carbon atoms in R group of a fatty acid may be 1 carbon to 19 carbon.

Question: A certain compound gives negative test with ninhydrin and positive test with Benedict’s solution. The compound is
Solution: Since, unknown compound gives a negative test with ninhydrin, it cannot be a protein or an amino acid. Since, it gives a positive test with Benedict's solution, it must be a monosaccharide but not a lipid.

Question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true ?
Options: Every individual has unique fingerprints and it occur at the tips of fingers
A sequence of bases of DNA is also unique for a person and information regarding this is called fingerprinting
Fingerprints can be altered by the surgery
Solution: Every individual has unique fingerprint. These occur at the tips of fingers and have been used for identification for long time but these are altered by surgery. A sequence of bases on DNA is also unique for person and information regarding this is called DNA finger printing.

Question: Recognize the figure and find out the correct matching.
Solution: The first figure represents the amino acid glycine. Figure (b) represents amino acid alanine whereas figure (c) represents the amino acid serine.

Question: Proteins are condensation polymers of
Solution:
The condensation polymers are the type of polymers which are formed by a condensation reaction where the molecules joined together and produce small molecules as by-products. Protein are the condensation polymers of $\alpha $-amino acids.
Hence, the correct answer is option (a)

Question: Anabolic pathways …a… energy and catabolic pathways …b… energy.
Solution: Metabolic pathways can lead to the formation of a more complex structure from a simpler structure. For example, conversion of acetic acid to cholesterol. Such a pathway is called anabolic pathways or biosynthetic pathways. Anabolic pathways, as expected, consume energy to synthesize larger complex molecules.  Metabolic pathways can lead to the formation of a simpler structure from a complex structure. For example, glucose becomes lactic acid in a skeletal muscle. Such a pathway is called degradation or catabolic pathway. Catabolic pathways lead to the release of energy due to the breakdown of molecules into simpler units.

Question: Glucose is a/an
Solution:
Glucose\[\left( {{C_6}{H_{12}}{O_6}} \right)\]contains aldehydic group

Hence aldohexose.

Question: The pH of blood does not appreciably change by a small addition of acid or a base because blood
Options: contains serum protein which acts as buffer
contains iron as a part of the molecule
Solution: Our blood consists of serum which is a protein. The buffer system present in blood is \[{H_2}C{O_3} + NaHC{O_3}\]. A buffer solution resists the change occurred in pH. Therefore, pH value of blood does not change by a small addition of any acid or a base.

Question: Which one of the following chemical units is certainly to be found in enzyme ?
Solution: Enzymes are mainly proteins which catalyze biological reactions following lock and key mechanism. It mainly consists of polypeptide bonds.

Question: Which one of the following is not a protein?
Solution:
Among the given options DNA i.e. deoxyribonucleic acid is not a protein. DNA is a nucleic acid while all other options are proteins.
Hence, the correct answer is option (d)

Question: \[{\text{C}}{{\text{H}}_{\text{3}}}{\left( {{\text{C}}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}} \right)_{{\text{14}}}}{\text{COOH}}\] is chemical formula of
Solution:
\[{\text{C}}{{\text{H}}_{\text{3}}}{\left( {{\text{C}}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}} \right)_{{\text{14}}}}{\text{COOH}}\]may be a formula of palmitic acid or hexadecanoic acid. Hexadecanoic acid may be a saturated long-chain fatty acid with a 16-carbon backbone.
